concerns.text: Tasks T037–T041 implement 'Fractal Dimension' and 'Radial Symmetry' metrics to address a reviewer comment, but these features are not defined in spec.md (which explicitly limits metrics to branch points, length, soma area, and Sholl counts) nor in plan.md. This creates unapproved scope creep; there is no task mapping for these new metrics to any existing FR/SC, and they introduce requirements not present in the source specification.

T008 ('Configure error handling to skip images...') and T017 ('Implement logic to skip empty fields...') are duplicate efforts for the same edge case defined in spec.md Edge Cases. T008 is a vague configuration task that does not produce a verifiable artifact, while T017 is the implementation. This redundancy risks implementation confusion and leaves the 'configuration' aspect of T008 without a clear spec-mapped task if T008 is removed.

SC-001 requires verifying extraction accuracy against a 'manually annotated subset' with <10% error. While T010 tests accuracy, the plan explicitly states no verified manual annotations exist and validation relies on synthetic data. There is no task defined to generate, manage, or run against a *real* manually annotated subset if one becomes available, leaving the success criterion dependent on synthetic data which may not satisfy the 'manual ground truth' requirement in a real-world scenario.

Tasks T037–T041 reference 'Turing Review' and 'Alan Turing' as the source of requirements. These IDs/references do not exist in the provided spec.md, plan.md, or constitution.md. These are stale tags referencing an external or non-existent artifact, violating the Single Source of Truth principle for task traceability.
